Arriving at a bus station to flee the city, PERSON calls GPE to gloat, but PERSON reveals that he anticipated his betrayal: he had already planned a route through the sewers to evade pursuit, and PERSON opens the case to discover a scrap metal decoy; ORG still has the real sceptre. widgetData.source_sentence: After a series of mysterious disappearances begin to plague a train, ORG multiple attempts to remedy the problem prove fruitless. sentences: In April 1967, the office of PERSON, a Jewish civil rights lawyer in GPE, GPE, is bombed by ORG, killing PERSON's five-year-old twin sons and leading to the amputation of PERSON's legs and his later suicide. PERSON refuses to authorize PERSON to seek access to ORG files, sealed by order of the state legislature, fearing it would expose PERSON's former associates in the ORG, as well allowing the Governor to obtain useful information on political enemies, which he indicates is the Governor's real reason for seeking PERSON's cooperation in unsealing the files. At the same time, PERSON, faced with the unearthed ghosts of the family history and having lapsed back into full-blown alcoholism, reveals to PERSON that in the early 1950s, as children, she and PERSON's father had witnessed their father murder the family's African-American neighbor PERSON during a fight that had started because PERSON's father, PERSON, had wrongly accused ORG's son of stealing a toy soldier. PERSON had blamed himself for the murder, as well as PERSON, for failing to stop PERSON, the guilt of which was a factor in GPE's later suicide and PERSON's alcoholism. PERSON also reveals how their father had been indoctrinated into the PERSON as a child, showing PERSON a historic photograph of PERSON as a young boy attending a ORG lynching which PERSON uses in his arguments before the courts. PERSON and PERSON secretly gain access to ORG sealed files, which prove a wider conspiracy to the bombing, and also indicate the participation of an accomplice.  The former ORG agent resurfaces, and reveals to PERSON that the ORG had identified the accomplice, PERSON, whom the ORG had never been able to prove responsible, but who has reunited with other ORG members to commemorate the bombing on the eve of the pending execution. PERSON goes to a ORG reunion but is beaten by several members and threatened at gunpoint by GPE. PERSON's persistence, the revelation of how much PERSON's hatred had destroyed his family, and his impending execution begin to affect PERSON, and he softens, reconciling with PERSON and expressing remorse for his actions and the effect they have had on his family. PERSON forcefully rejects the ORG when Wedge visits him in prison to encourage him to remain silent, and it is revealed that GPE was the one who had built the bomb and set it deliberately to detonate. Ultimately, PERSON's motions for a stay are denied by the courts, including ORG. Despite PERSON's finally authorizing the release of relevant Sovereignty Commission files, the Governor refuses to grant clemency, betraying him and PERSON, while nonetheless using the files as political leverage as PERSON had predicted. GPE, identified in the files, is finally arrested. Ultimately, PERSON is executed in the gas chamber, though PERSON remains a confidant and advocate for his grandfather up until his execution, and he and PERSON embrace at the end, in the hope that maybe the ghosts of the past are gone.

While Tanjiro and his friends mourn PERSON's death, the remaining PERSON receive the news and PERSON appreciates him for not letting a single passenger or comrade die in his presence, stating that he will be glad to reunite with him when he finally passes.
